setting.note: Hugo's Paris is the heart of the book, and he maps it street by street: the Gorbeau tenement on the Boulevard de l'Hôpital, the Luxembourg where Marius and Cosette fall in love, the convent of Petit-Picpus, the barricade of the Rue de la Chanvrerie by Les Halles, and the sewers Valjean carries the wounded Marius through. The story reaches it from far off, though: from Digne in the Alps, from Montreuil-sur-Mer in the north, from the battlefield of Waterloo.

rights.originalWork: Public domain. Victor Hugo, first published 1862; the author died in 1885.
rights.translation: English translation by Isabel F. Hapgood (1887); she died in 1928, so the translation is public domain in the US and UK/EU.
rights.territoryNotes: Public domain in the US and UK/EU: Hugo died 1885, Hapgood 1928.
rights.attribution: Victor Hugo, Les Misérables (1862), trans. Isabel F. Hapgood. Text from Project Gutenberg.

palette.accentSourceQuote: nothing was to be seen except the red flag
palette.note: The red flag of the June 1832 barricade, the one image the whole novel drives toward. A deep signal red that clears WCAG AA on paper.

marius.name: Marius Pontmercy
marius.colorSourceQuote: he had not put off his black garments
